column5: MCP tool handler boundary: any exception is converted into a structured error result returned to the client rather than propagated across the protocol.

Fire-and-forget MCP notification: errors are intentionally swallowed so a failed list-changed notification cannot crash the server.
Health-check ping loop: any ping failure is treated uniformly to trigger reconnection; the loop must not terminate on an unexpected exception type.
LSP request-dispatch boundary: uncaught exceptions are converted into a JSON-RPC InternalError response per the LSP spec rather than propagated.
LSP message-loop boundary: the server logs and continues so a single malformed message cannot terminate the session.
Top-level demo entry point loading external, possibly corrupt save data; recovers by reporting and exiting. Explanatory comment retained per project precedent.
Top-level demo entry point wrapping windowing/graphics init, which can surface many platform exceptions (no display, driver, GL context); recovers by reporting and exiting. Explanatory comment added.

The upcast fixes the projected tuple element type to IEnumerable<MenuItemDef> so the sequence matches CreateMenuBar's parameter; ValueTuple is invariant, so removing it (leaving MenuItemDef[]) breaks the call.
